solution.p: We will factor t^{2}-18t+80 where a=1, b=-18, and c=80.
Mental Method: When we factor a polynomial in the form of x^2+bx+c, x^2+bxy+cy^2, or x^2y^2+bxy+c we can use mental math to factor. We need to find two numbers whose product is c, and whose sum is b.

For t^{2}-18t+80, since the product of -8 and -10 is 80, and the sum of -8 and -10 is -18, the factorization is \begin{aligned}[t]t^{2}-18t+80\amp =\left(t-8\right)\mathopen{}\left(t-10\right)\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (1) \cdot (80) = 80.

The number 80 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(80)\amp -1(-80)\amp 2(40)\amp -2(-40)
ewline4(20)\amp -4(-20)\amp 5(16)\amp -5(-16)
ewline8(10)\amp -8(-10)\amp \end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of -18, and this sum happens with (-10)+(-8)=-18 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]t^{2}-18t+80\amp =t^{2}-10t-8t+80
ewline \amp =t\mathopen{}\left(t-10ight)-8\mathopen{}\left(t-10ight)
ewline \amp =\left(t-8ight)\mathopen{}\left(t-10ight)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in t^{2}-18t+80 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 1, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(1)\amp \end{array}

The third term, 80, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(80)\amp -1(-80)\amp 2(40)\amp -2(-40)\newline4(20)\amp -4(-20)\amp 5(16)\amp -5(-16)\newline8(10)\amp -8(-10)\amp \end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with t^{2}-18t+80 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]t^{2}-18t+80\amp =\left(t-8\right)\mathopen{}\left(t-10\right)\end{aligned}
